areas.name: Punta Cana & Bavaro
areas.summary: The Caribbean's all-inclusive capital: a 30-mile ribbon of palm-lined white sand engineered for stress-free, sun-soaked resort holidays.
areas.description:
Punta Cana and neighboring Bavaro sit on the easternmost tip of the Dominican Republic in La Altagracia province, fronting a near-continuous stretch of powder-white beach where the warm turquoise Caribbean meets the Atlantic. Bavaro Beach itself runs roughly 12 miles between Cabeza de Toro and Macao, lined wall-to-wall with all-inclusive mega-resorts, and is fringed by a protective offshore coral reef that keeps the water calm and shallow — ideal for swimming and families. This is the Dominican Republic's biggest and most developed tourism hub, purpose-built for vacationers who want everything (food, drinks, pools, watersports, entertainment) bundled and walk-out-the-door access to the sand.

The area suits beach lovers, families, couples, honeymooners, and groups chasing an easy, low-friction tropical getaway rather than independent cultural exploration. Beyond the beach, the headline attraction is Scape Park at Cap Cana, home to the photogenic Hoyo Azul cenote (a turquoise natural sinkhole), ziplines, and caves. Popular day trips include catamaran sails to the protected white-sand paradise of Isla Saona (in Cotubanama National Park), the wild and surf-friendly Macao Beach, the upscale Juanillo Beach and marina at Cap Cana, and the freshwater lagoons of the Indigenous Eyes Ecological Reserve. Nightlife centers on resort shows, beach bars, and the famous Coco Bongo club.

Getting there is exceptionally easy: Punta Cana International (PUJ) — the busiest airport in the country, with direct flights from across North America and Europe — is only about 20 km from the Bavaro hotel zone, a 20–40 minute drive depending on traffic and which resort you're heading to. The trade-off for all this convenience is authenticity: the resort corridor feels manicured and international, local Dominican culture is thin on the ground, and the east-facing beaches are the most exposed in the country to seasonal sargassum seaweed.
highlights: Bavaro Beach — 12 miles of calm, reef-protected white sand
Hoyo Azul cenote & Scape Park at Cap Cana (ziplines, caves)
Isla Saona day trip by catamaran (Cotubanama National Park)
Macao Beach — wild, scenic, the area's main surf spot
Juanillo Beach & Cap Cana Marina (upscale, sargassum-sheltered)
Indigenous Eyes Ecological Reserve — 12 freshwater lagoons
Coco Bongo Punta Cana nightclub & show
Catamaran party cruises and the natural pools off the coast
pros: Stunning, swimmable reef-protected beaches with reliably warm, calm water
Huge range of all-inclusive resorts for every budget and family-friendly amenities
Easiest access in the DR — busy international airport just 20–40 min away
Very high English prevalence and strong, tourist-focused safety/security
Great base for day trips (Saona, Macao, Scape Park)
cons: High sargassum/seaweed risk on east-facing beaches, worst June–August
Little authentic Dominican culture — feels manicured and international
Low walkability; you rely on resort shuttles, taxis, or tours to get around
Off-resort prices and excursions can feel overpriced; tourist-trap pressure
Peak-season (Dec–Mar) and holiday rates climb steeply

areas.name: Cap Cana
areas.summary: A polished, gated luxury enclave south of Punta Cana with the area's most sheltered beach, a glamorous marina, and a world-class Jack Nicklaus golf course.
areas.description:
Cap Cana is a 30,000-acre master-planned, gated community on the Dominican Republic's far east coast, just south of Punta Cana and only about a 10-15 minute drive from Punta Cana International Airport (PUJ). Built around soaring coral cliffs, tropical forest, the Caribbean Sea, and a yacht-filled marina, it's the most upscale corner of the Punta Cana region, designed as a self-contained resort destination rather than a town with everyday local life.

Its crown jewel is Juanillo Beach (Playa Juanillo), a wide arc of white sand and calm turquoise water lined with coconut palms. Because it sits in a protected cove and is shielded by an offshore boom, it's generally one of the least sargassum-affected and best-groomed beaches in the area. The beach is technically public but reached through the gated community; non-resort visitors typically pay a roughly USD 20-25 fee that doubles as a food-and-beverage credit at the Juanillo beach club. Marina Cap Cana anchors the dining and nightlife scene with oceanfront restaurants, sportfishing, yacht charters, and Api Beach, while Punta Espada is a Jack Nicklaus Signature course repeatedly ranked the best in the Caribbean.

Cap Cana suits luxury travelers, honeymooners, golfers, yacht and fishing enthusiasts, and families who want top-tier all-inclusive resorts (Hyatt Ziva and Zilara, Secrets, Sanctuary, Eden Roc, the St. Regis) in a secure, manicured setting. It is not the place for budget travelers, backpackers, or those seeking authentic local culture and street life. Getting there is easy: PUJ is one of the busiest airports in the Caribbean with abundant nonstop flights from North America and Europe, and the resort is a short, well-signed transfer away.
areas.whale_season: N/A — humpbacks are seen in Samaná Bay (mid-Jan to mid-Mar), a separate region on the northeast coast
highlights: Juanillo Beach (Playa Juanillo) — sheltered white-sand cove with a beach club
Marina Cap Cana — yachts, oceanfront dining, sportfishing and Api Beach

areas.name: Puerto Plata, Sosua & Cabarete
areas.summary: The Dominican Republic's adventure-and-watersports coast, pairing kitesurf-mecca Cabarete with old-town Puerto Plata, a mountain cable car, and waterfall canyoning.
areas.description:
The North Coast stretches along the Atlantic from the historic port city of Puerto Plata east through the dive-and-beach town of Sosúa to Cabarete, the Caribbean's kitesurfing and windsurfing capital. Backed by the green slopes of Mount Isabel de Torres and cooled by steady trade winds, the region feels more rugged and active than the polished mega-resorts of the south. Puerto Plata itself blends faded Victorian architecture, the 16th-century Fortaleza San Felipe, an amber museum, and a long seaside malecón, while the gated Playa Dorada complex concentrates the area's all-inclusive resorts and golf.

The beaches here are varied rather than uniformly postcard-perfect: Playa Dorada and Costa Dorada offer calm golden sand for families, Sosúa Bay is a sheltered crescent prized for snorkeling and diving, Playa Encuentro is the coast's premier surf break, and Cabarete's wind-raked bay draws kiters and windsurfers from around the world. Because the coast faces north and northwest, its waters are generally clearer of the sargassum seaweed that plagues the country's eastern and southeastern shores.

The area suits active travelers, watersports enthusiasts, and budget-conscious visitors who want culture and adventure alongside beach time, more than those seeking a purely manicured luxury bubble. Getting there is easy: Gregorio Luperón International Airport (POP) sits right on the coast between the towns, roughly 10 minutes from Playa Dorada and Sosúa and about 30 minutes from Cabarete, with direct flights from North America and Europe.
areas.whale_season: Mid-Jan to late Mar — humpbacks in Samaná Bay (2-3 hr day trip)
highlights: Cabarete Bay & Kite Beach — Caribbean kitesurfing/windsurfing capital
Mount Isabel de Torres cable car — Caribbean's only aerial tramway to a Christ the Redeemer statue and botanical garden

Playa Encuentro — the North Coast's top surf break

areas.name: Samana & Las Terrenas
areas.summary: A lush, mountainous Caribbean peninsula where humpback whales breed in winter, jungle waterfalls tumble to swimmable pools, and a French-Italian beach town fronts some of the country's most beautiful sand.
areas.description:
The Samana Peninsula juts off the Dominican Republic's northeast coast, a green spine of coconut-covered hills and palm-backed beaches between Samana Bay and the Atlantic. Las Terrenas, on the north shore, is its lively heart: a former fishing village transformed by decades of European settlement into a walkable, cosmopolitan resort town where nearly half the population is expat (French, Italian, German and others). Across the peninsula sit laid-back Las Galeras and the bayside town of Santa Barbara de Samana, the launch point for the famous winter whale-watching trips.

The area is best known for nature and atmosphere rather than mega-resorts. Samana Bay hosts thousands of North Atlantic humpback whales from mid-January to late March, the 40-meter El Limon waterfall draws hikers and horseback riders, and Cayo Levantado ("Bacardi Island") offers postcard-perfect day trips. The beaches are exceptional: Playa Rincon near Las Galeras is regularly ranked among the Caribbean's finest, while Playa Bonita, Playa Coson and the town beach at Las Terrenas deliver soft sand, calm reef-protected water and excellent French-Italian dining and beach bars.

It suits travelers who want a more authentic, boutique and outdoorsy alternative to Punta Cana — couples, foodies, nature lovers, divers and longer-stay/digital-nomad visitors. Getting there is easiest via Samana El Catey (AZS), roughly 35 minutes from Las Terrenas; many visitors also drive about 2 to 2.5 hours from Santo Domingo's airports (SDQ) on the modern toll highway, or arrive on day trips from Punta Cana. A car or scooter is handy, as attractions are spread across the peninsula.
areas.whale_season: Mid-Jan to end of Mar (official 2026 season Jan 15-Mar 31) — humpbacks breeding in Samana Bay; February is peak

areas.name: La Romana & Bayahibe
areas.summary: A laid-back southeastern beach base with Blue Flag sands, the Caribbean's best-protected coast against seaweed, and boat rides to picture-perfect Saona and Catalina islands.
areas.description:
La Romana and the neighboring fishing village of Bayahibe sit on the Dominican Republic's southeast coast, in La Altagracia Province along the Caribbean Sea. The compact zone bundles three distinct experiences: working-town La Romana (home to its eponymous international airport, LRM), the exclusive 7,000-acre Casa de Campo megaresort with its three championship golf courses and the cliff-top Mediterranean-style artisan village of Altos de Chavón, and the relaxed beach hamlet of Bayahibe with its all-inclusive resort strip around Playa Dominicus. It is the gateway to Cotubanamá National Park (Parque Nacional del Este) and the two most famous offshore day trips in the country.

Bayahibe's beaches are a genuine highlight: Playa Dominicus holds Blue Flag status and the old fishing-village beach has soft white sand and calm, clear, swimmable water. Crucially, the bay's geography and active beach management keep it roughly 97-99 percent sargassum-free even in bad seaweed years, making it one of the few Caribbean spots that reliably dodges the problem that plagues nearby Punta Cana. From Bayahibe's harbor, catamarans and speedboats run to Saona Island (palm-fringed beaches, starfish-filled natural pools and mangroves) and Catalina Island, a top snorkeling and scuba-diving site with a wall dive and shallow reef aquarium.

The area suits travelers who want a quieter, more grounded alternative to Punta Cana: couples, divers and snorkelers, families wanting calm water, golfers, and culture-curious visitors drawn to Altos de Chavón's amphitheater, galleries and amber/larimar museum. Getting there is easy — LRM airport is only about 20 minutes from Bayahibe and 5-10 minutes from Casa de Campo, and Punta Cana International (PUJ) is roughly a 60-75 minute drive for those flying into the busier hub. Santo Domingo is about two hours west.
areas.whale_season: N/A — humpback whale watching is in Samaná Bay on the northeast coast, roughly mid-January to mid-March

areas.name: Santo Domingo
areas.summary: The Americas' oldest European city — a buzzing capital where 500-year-old cobblestone streets meet Caribbean street life, not a beach resort.
areas.description:
Santo Domingo is the capital of the Dominican Republic and the largest metropolitan area in the Caribbean, sitting on the country's south coast at the mouth of the Ozama River where it meets the Caribbean Sea. Its heart is the Zona Colonial (Ciudad Colonial), a UNESCO World Heritage site and the oldest permanent European settlement in the Americas — founded in 1498. The compact, walkable colonial quarter packs in the first cathedral, first paved street, first fortress and first hospital of the New World within roughly eleven blocks of cobblestone and Spanish-baroque architecture.

This is a history, food and culture destination rather than a beach holiday. Travelers come for the Catedral Primada de América, the Alcázar de Colón, Calle Las Damas and Fortaleza Ozama, the lively Parque Colón and El Conde pedestrian street, plus the seaside Malecón (Av. George Washington) for sunset strolls, nightlife and merengue. It suits culture seekers, history buffs, city-break and solo travelers, foodies and digital nomads more than sun-and-sand seekers.

The city has no real swimming beaches of its own — the closest are Boca Chica and Juan Dolio, roughly 30-50 minutes east, which can suffer seaweed in summer. Getting there is easy: Las Américas International Airport (SDQ) is about 28 km east, a 30-45 minute drive via the Autopista Las Américas, and the city has a clean, cheap metro plus reliable Uber. The Zona Colonial is patrolled by dedicated tourist police (CESTUR) and is very pleasant on foot by day, though it quiets down after dark.
areas.whale_season: N/A — humpback whales are seen in Samaná Bay (far north), not Santo Domingo

areas.name: Barahona
areas.summary: The Dominican Republic's wild, undiscovered southwest, home to the impossibly pristine Bahía de las Águilas and the world's only larimar mines.
areas.description:
Barahona is the gateway to the Dominican Republic's rugged southwest, a province nicknamed the "Pearl of the South" where the Sierra de Bahoruco mountains plunge straight into the Caribbean. This is off-the-beaten-path DR: dramatic pebble-and-sand coves backed by cliffs, cold mountain rivers spilling onto the beach, arid cactus valleys, and a salt lake below sea level. It is one of the country's leading ecotourism hubs and feels worlds away from the all-inclusive resort strips of the east.

The region's crown jewel is Bahía de las Águilas, a roughly 8 km stretch of undeveloped white sand and crystalline turquoise water inside Jaragua National Park (part of a UNESCO biosphere reserve), reached only by 4x4 or boat from Cabo Rojo. Barahona is also the only place on Earth where larimar — the rare sky-blue gemstone — is mined, up in the Bahoruco highlands above La Ciénaga. Other draws include the San Rafael and Los Patos river-fed beaches, Lago Enriquillo (the Caribbean's largest lake, hypersaline and home to crocodiles and iguanas), and a scenic coastal highway south of town.

It suits adventurous travelers, nature lovers, road-trippers and photographers willing to trade resort comforts for raw scenery and authentic small-town Dominican life. Most beaches here are dramatic and rocky rather than swim-up resort sand, and infrastructure is modest. Getting there means flying into Santo Domingo (SDQ) — the local María Montez airport (BRX) has no scheduled commercial flights — then driving roughly 3 to 3.5 hours southwest via Autopista 6 de Noviembre and Highway 44.
areas.whale_season: N/A — humpback whale watching is in Samaná Bay on the northeast coast, not the southwest

hotels.name: Hard Rock Hotel & Casino Punta Cana
hotels.summary: A vast, music-themed all-inclusive mega-resort with the largest casino in the Dominican Republic, a Nicklaus golf course and 13 pools, geared to families and party-minded couples alike.
hotels.description: Sprawling across 121 acres of Macao Beach north of Bavaro, Hard Rock Hotel & Casino Punta Cana is one of the largest all-inclusive resorts in the Dominican Republic, with roughly 1,882 suites spread over multiple low-rise buildings. The rock-and-roll branding runs throughout, from the on-site Vegas-style casino (the biggest in the region) and Oro nightclub to in-room hydro spa tubs and a guitar-lending program. Thirteen pools, three swim-up bars, a lazy river, the Rockaway Bay water park and the Jack Nicklaus-designed Hard Rock Golf Club at Cana Bay keep guests of all ages busy.
pros: Enormous range of pools, restaurants and entertainment in one resort
On-site casino, nightclub and water park suit both families and couples

Jack Nicklaus golf course at Cana Bay
cons: Very large footprint means a lot of walking and crowds at peak times

hotels.name: Lopesan Costa Bavaro Resort, Spa & Casino
hotels.summary: A modern, design-forward all-inclusive on wide Bavaro Beach combining a striking double-level infinity pool, a casino and a lively entertainment boulevard.
hotels.description: Opened in 2019, Lopesan Costa Bavaro is one of the newer large-scale resorts on Bavaro Beach and one of the most architecturally contemporary, built around a landmark two-tier infinity pool. The 'Boulevard' leisure district packs in a casino, a large amphitheater, a nightclub and a row of bars, while several themed restaurants and two large buffets cover the dining. The wide, soft-sand beach and proximity to the airport make it a strong mainstream all-inclusive pick for couples and families.

hotels.name: Excellence Punta Cana
hotels.summary: An elegant adults-only, all-suite all-inclusive on a quiet stretch of Uvero Alto beach, known for fine dining, an excellent spa and a calmer, romance-focused vibe.
hotels.description: Set on the more secluded Uvero Alto coast about 45 minutes north of the airport, Excellence Punta Cana is an adults-only (18+), all-suite retreat aimed squarely at couples and honeymooners. Every suite has a furnished balcony or terrace, marble bathroom and a jetted whirlpool tub, and many are swim-up. With around 11 restaurants, a standout spa and hydrotherapy circuit, and a long, uncrowded beach, it trades the casino-and-nightclub energy of Bavaro for a quieter, more refined experience.

hotels.name: Barcelo Bavaro Palace
hotels.summary: A large, value-oriented family all-inclusive on Bavaro Beach with two water parks, an 18-hole P.B. Dye golf course, a casino and a bowling alley for non-stop activity.
hotels.description: Part of the big Barcelo Bavaro complex, Barcelo Bavaro Palace is built for families and groups who want plenty to do without leaving the resort. Its biggest draws are two water parks - the kid-focused Barcy and the Pirates Island wave-pool park - plus an 18-hole P.B. Dye golf course, casino, bowling alley, theater and dedicated Family Club rooms with two bedrooms. The beach is a broad, classic Bavaro stretch, and the all-inclusive pricing tends to undercut the flashier neighbors.

hotels.name: JOIA Bavaro by Iberostar
hotels.summary: An intimate adults-only, all-suite luxury resort (formerly Iberostar Grand Bavaro) with butler service, in-suite hot tubs and a calmer feel on the Arena Gorda beachfront.
hotels.description: Rebranded from Iberostar Grand Bavaro to JOIA Bavaro in mid-2024, this adults-only (18+) all-suite property sits on the Arena Gorda/Bavaro beachfront and is the most upscale, intimate corner of the wider Iberostar complex. With around 272 suites - each with a private hot tub - plus butler and concierge service, three ocean-view pools and a replica Spanish galleon serving as one of its bars, it leans into a quieter, romance-focused luxury experience. Guests also have access to the adjacent P.B. Dye golf course.

hotels.name: Majestic Mirage Punta Cana
hotels.summary: An all-suite Arena Gorda resort that splits into a 5-building adults-only Mirage Club and a 2-building Family Club, with multiple pools and consistently high guest ratings.
hotels.description: Majestic Mirage is an all-suite all-inclusive on Playa Arena Gorda that cleverly serves two audiences at once: five buildings form the adults-only (18+) Mirage Club, separated by landscaping from two Family Club buildings that welcome children. Every suite includes a jacuzzi and butler service, and the resort spreads multiple pools and four swim-up bars across the grounds. Praised for cleanliness and service, it sits on the Bavaro/Arena Gorda beachfront and offers a quieter, more boutique-feeling stay than the largest neighbors while still covering both couples and families.

Multiple pools and several swim-up bars

hotels.name: Hyatt Ziva Cap Cana
amenities: Water park with lazy river and slides
hotels.summary: Sprawling family-friendly all-inclusive on Juanillo Beach with a big water park and dual-brand access to the adults-only Zilara side.
hotels.description: Hyatt Ziva Cap Cana is the family half of a dual-branded 750-room mega-resort set on oceanfront within the gated Cap Cana enclave on Juanillo Beach. It has 375 contemporary oceanfront suites, a complimentary on-site water park with a lazy river and slides, a large kids club, swim-up suites and access to roughly two dozen shared food and beverage outlets across the Ziva and Zilara properties. It consistently ranks among the top resorts in the Dominican Republic on Tripadvisor (ranked #1 in the 2025 Travelers' Choice Best of the Best).

hotels.summary: Adults-only oceanfront all-inclusive on Juanillo Beach that earned Tripadvisor's 2026 #1 Travelers' Choice ranking.

It shares the oceanfront site and roughly 25 food and beverage outlets with the family-oriented Ziva while keeping its pools, beach zones and entertainment exclusively for guests 18 and over. It was ranked #1 in Tripadvisor's 2026 Travelers' Choice Best of the Best awards.

hotels.summary: Adults-only Unlimited-Luxury all-inclusive on Juanillo Beach with multiple dining venues, a spa and a nightclub.
hotels.description: Secrets Cap Cana Resort & Spa is a 457-room adults-only resort on the white sand of Juanillo Beach inside the gated Cap Cana community, run under Hyatt's Inclusive Collection with the Unlimited-Luxury concept (no reservations needed at a-la-carte restaurants, top-shelf spirits and 24-hour room service included). It offers around eight dining options, a Pevonia spa, a nightclub, an indoor theater and a Preferred Club tier with an exclusive pool and lounge.

hotels.name: Eden Roc Cap Cana
hotels.summary: Intimate ultra-luxury Relais & Chateaux retreat of around 68 private suites and villas, the first Dominican hotel with Two MICHELIN Keys.
hotels.description: Eden Roc Cap Cana is the only Relais & Chateaux property in the Dominican Republic and the first to earn Two MICHELIN Keys (awarded October 2025). The intimate resort comprises roughly 68 stand-alone pool suites, beachfront suites, oceanfront bungalows and villas, set among gardens and lagoon-style pools with a private Blue Flag beach club and a 20,000-square-foot wellness and spa center. It is a food-driven, highly personalized luxury experience served on a bed-and-breakfast basis rather than a large all-inclusive.

hotels.summary: Adults-only Luxury Collection all-inclusive built like a colonial Spanish castle town beside the Cap Cana marina.

The resort has six pools, multiple a-la-carte restaurants, a live piano bar and an adjacent Sanctuary Town with extra dining and shops.

amenities: Member of Small Luxury Hotels of the World
hotels.summary: An intimate 50-suite five-star boutique hotel on Playa Dorada, blending restored colonial elegance with a chic rooftop infinity pool and award-winning spa.
hotels.description: Casa Colonial is the North Coast's most refined small luxury property, a member of Small Luxury Hotels of the World set on a meticulously restored colonial estate at the edge of Playa Dorada. Its 50 all-suite accommodations, rooftop infinity pool with jacuzzis, the Bagua Spa, and the French-Caribbean fine-dining restaurant Lucia draw couples and design-minded travelers who want quiet sophistication rather than a sprawling all-inclusive megaresort. It is the rare Puerto Plata hotel that competes on style and service rather than volume.

hotels.summary: A large, relatively modern five-star family all-inclusive on Maimon Bay near Amber Cove, with extensive gardens, multiple pools and varied dining.
hotels.description: Senator Puerto Plata Spa Resort is one of the newer large-scale all-inclusives on the North Coast, set on a golden-sand beach in Bahia de Maimon close to the Amber Cove cruise port. Its 567 rooms, several specialty restaurants plus buffet and cafeteria, multiple pools with jacuzzis, a large Senzia spa and lush landscaped gardens make it a strong family and couples option that feels fresher than the older Playa Dorada stock. Its slightly more remote bay location is a trade-off for the upgraded, recently remodeled facilities.

hotels.summary: An intimate, European-styled luxury beachfront resort of suites and villas on quiet Playa Coson, and a member of Small Luxury Hotels of the World.
hotels.description: Sublime Samana sits on the palm-fringed sands of Playa Coson, one of the most tranquil and scenic stretches near Las Terrenas, and its layout of one- to three-bedroom apartments and villas (each with a full kitchen and terrace) makes it feel more like a refined residential enclave than a big resort. hotels.description: Viva V Samana by Wyndham (a Trademark Collection adults-only resort) is the peninsula's social, value-minded all-inclusive aimed at couples and groups of friends, set on more than a kilometer of Playa Coson beachfront. It is a strong mid-range pick for travelers who want all-inclusive convenience without going fully luxury, and it sits unusually close to AZS airport (about 20-25 minutes).

hotels.description: Hotel Villa Serena is a tranquil, individually-decorated boutique hotel set in a lush seaside garden in Las Galeras, at the eastern tip of the peninsula. It is the ideal base for reaching Playa Rincon (a short boat ride or scenic drive away), consistently regarded as one of the Caribbean's most beautiful beaches.

hotels.name: Casa de Campo Resort & Villas
hotels.summary: A sprawling 7,000-acre luxury golf and villa resort that is the most prestigious address in La Romana, home to the famed Teeth of the Dog course.
hotels.description: Casa de Campo is the grande dame of the Dominican southeast: a 7,000-acre private estate combining a hotel, luxury villas, three Pete Dye championship golf courses (including the world-ranked Teeth of the Dog), a marina, equestrian center, shooting range and the artisan village of Altos de Chavon. Rooms feature mahogany furnishings and private terraces, and the resort's Minitas Beach Club fronts a sheltered Caribbean cove. It is a polished, service-oriented retreat that draws golfers, families and well-heeled travelers rather than the all-inclusive party crowd. Note that it operates on a room-only or breakfast basis with most dining, golf and activities charged separately, so the true cost runs well above the headline rate. Its enormous footprint means you'll rely on a golf cart to get around.
pros: World-class golf with three championship courses
Vast range of activities (marina, riding, shooting, tennis)
Just 10 minutes from La Romana airport

hotels.name: Dreams Dominicus La Romana
hotels.summary: An upscale Hyatt-managed beachfront all-inclusive on Bayahibe's powder-white sand, balancing family Explorer's Club fun with adults-oriented Preferred Club perks.
hotels.description: Dreams Dominicus La Romana sits on a wide, calm stretch of Bayahibe beach about 20 minutes from La Romana airport, backed by lush tropical vegetation. Part of Hyatt's Inclusive Collection, it offers the brand's Unlimited-Luxury all-inclusive: multiple restaurants with no reservations required, a spa, PADI dive center, four pools and a strong kids' and teens' program, making it a genuine all-ages resort. Preferred Club guests get an upgraded lounge, beach area and swim-up suites. The Caribbean-side location means clearer, calmer water and superb sunsets compared with the Atlantic-facing Punta Cana resorts. It is large and can feel busy at peak times, and reaching the more distant suites involves a fair walk, but it is consistently well rated for food variety and beach quality.

Strong family programming plus adults-oriented Preferred Club
Multiple restaurants with no reservations needed
On-site spa and PADI dive center
cons: Large resort that can feel crowded at peak season
Some suites are a long walk from the beach and lobby
Sargassum can affect the beach in summer months
Premium experiences (steakhouse, spa) sit behind upsells

hotels.name: Secrets La Romana Resort & Spa
hotels.summary: The adults-only Bayahibe beachfront resort formerly branded Hilton La Romana, now Hyatt's Secrets La Romana: a calm, modern all-inclusive for couples with a large spa and swim-out rooms.
hotels.description: Secrets La Romana Resort & Spa occupies the tranquil adults-only side of a large twin resort on Bayahibe beach, sharing grounds with a family-focused sister property (now Dreams La Romana) via covered walkways but reserving its own pools, restaurants and beach for guests 18 and over. The resort was formerly Hilton La Romana, an All-Inclusive Adult Only Resort, and rebranded under Hyatt's Secrets brand in June 2025 following Hyatt's acquisition of Playa Hotels & Resorts. A 2019 renovation brought a contemporary look with rainfall showers, free minibars and furnished terraces, some with swim-out access. Dining spans nine venues from Pan-Asian to seafood, and the 21,000-square-foot spa is among the largest in the region. The Caribbean-facing beach is calm and the vibe is relaxed rather than raucous, which suits couples seeking a quieter all-inclusive close to La Romana airport.

Large 21,000 sq ft spa and modern renovated rooms
Swim-out room options and a quiet beachfront
Hyatt Inclusive Collection Unlimited-Luxury service
cons: Shares grounds with a busy family resort
Sargassum can affect the beach in summer
Sprawling layout means some long walks
Restaurant reservations can be hard to secure at peak times

hotels.name: Iberostar Selection Hacienda Dominicus
hotels.summary: A family-friendly, colonial-style all-inclusive on Dominicus beach known for its dive center, coral conservation program and landmark windmill bar.
hotels.description: Iberostar Selection Hacienda Dominicus is a long-established 5-star all-inclusive set in colonial-style two-story buildings around landscaped courtyards on the gently sloping Dominicus beach. It is a favorite of divers thanks to the on-site Dressel Divers center, which runs PADI courses and trips to the St George shipwreck, Saona and Catalina, plus an in-water coral nursery as part of Iberostar's Wave of Change sustainability program. Families are well served by the Star Camp kids' program and a splash pool, while the iconic windmill-lighthouse bar is a signature sunset spot. The resort blends harmoniously into its gardens and has a relaxed, traditional feel; rooms and some facilities show their age in parts, but the beach, diving and entertainment keep loyal repeat guests coming back.

Attractive colonial architecture and mature gardens
Strong family entertainment and Star Camp kids' club
Blue Flag-quality Dominicus beach with calm water

Buffet quality can be inconsistent at peak times
Sargassum possible in summer
Wi-Fi and a-la-carte reservations can be patchy

hotels.name: Catalonia Bayahibe
hotels.summary: A relaxed, good-value all-inclusive set in villa-style buildings on a Blue Flag Bayahibe beach, popular with families and couples.
hotels.description: Catalonia Bayahibe is a mid-range, well-priced all-inclusive on one of the Dominican Republic's few Blue Flag-certified beaches, surrounded by leafy tropical gardens just outside the fishing village of Bayahibe. Run by Spain's Catalonia Hotels & Resorts, the property has around 415 rooms in low-rise, two-story villas integrated into the landscape, with room types ranging from comfort and family rooms with bunk beds to romantic deluxe rooms; the optional Privileged package adds an exclusive beach area, preferential restaurant access and other perks. Facilities include a large central pool with separate children's and jacuzzi areas, a spa, six restaurants, watersports and nightly entertainment. It offers a calmer, less crowded alternative to Punta Cana at a friendly price, though the food and entertainment are solid rather than spectacular and the single main pool can get busy.

Calm, low-rise villa layout in mature gardens
Family-friendly with bunk-bed rooms and kids' club
Close to Bayahibe village and boat trips to Saona/Catalina
cons: Only one main pool, which gets crowded
Food and entertainment are decent rather than standout
Some rooms feel dated
Sargassum can affect the beach in summer

hotels.name: Viva Dominicus Beach by Wyndham, A Trademark All Inclusive
hotels.summary: A lively, activity-packed and great-value family all-inclusive on Dominicus beach, famous for its seaside trapeze and circus school.
hotels.description: Viva Dominicus Beach by Wyndham is a large, energetic and budget-friendly all-inclusive spread along the white sand of Dominicus beach. Its standout feature is a packed activities program including a seaside trapeze and circus school, climbing wall, tennis, beach volleyball and a busy entertainment team, making it a hit with active families and younger travelers. There are four freshwater pools, six restaurants and access to spa services at the adjacent sister resort Viva Dominicus Palace. With 712 rooms it is one of the bigger and more affordable options in the area; rooms are simple and some are dated, and the high-energy, somewhat dated cruise-style vibe won't suit everyone, but for value, beach quality and non-stop activities it punches above its price.

Huge range of activities including a seaside trapeze
Lovely white-sand Dominicus beach with calm water
Great for active families and younger travelers

hotels.name: JW Marriott Hotel Santo Domingo
amenities: Connected to Blue Mall shopping center
hotels.summary: A polished 5-star business-and-leisure tower in the upscale Piantini district, directly connected to the Blue Mall shopping center.
hotels.description: The JW Marriott Hotel Santo Domingo sits in the prestigious Piantini financial and shopping district, physically attached to the high-end Blue Mall, making it a favorite for business travelers and luxury shoppers rather than beach-seekers. Its 150 rooms are modern and generously sized, with floor-to-ceiling windows offering city views, and the rooftop pool deck, Vertygo 101 lounge, and on-site spa give it a contemporary, urban-resort feel. It is roughly a 15-minute drive from the Zona Colonial. While the location is excellent for dining and nightlife in the modern part of the city, it is not walkable to the historic core, so guests prioritizing the Colonial Zone should expect short taxi rides. Food and beverage are charged separately as it operates room-only, and rates run high for the city.

Modern, spacious rooms with great city views
Rooftop pool, spa, and well-regarded rooftop lounge

hotels.name: El Embajador, a Royal Hideaway Hotel
hotels.summary: A historic 1950s landmark hotel set in lush gardens, restored by Barcelo's Royal Hideaway brand with a spa and casino.
hotels.description: Opened in the 1950s as one of Santo Domingo's grand hotels, El Embajador is a mid-century landmark on Avenida Sarasota in the residential Bella Vista neighborhood, surrounded by some of the largest private tropical gardens in the city. After a full restoration under Barcelo's upscale Royal Hideaway banner, the property offers nearly 300 modern, soundproofed rooms, a large outdoor pool, a full wellness spa, and an on-site casino, all in a quiet residential pocket between the modern city and the Malecon. It carries genuine local prestige and offers far more grounds and resort-style amenities than the boutique hotels of the Colonial Zone. The trade-off is location: it is a drive (about 10-15 minutes) from both the Zona Colonial and the seafront, and the mid-century bones mean some public areas feel formal and dated despite the renovation.

Large pool, full spa, and on-site casino
Spacious, quiet, soundproofed modern rooms
Strong value for a 5-star with full resort amenities

Residential setting means fewer dining options right outside

hotels.name: Catalonia Santo Domingo
hotels.summary: A 21-story tower on the Malecon with Caribbean Sea views from every room, a casino, and a rooftop pool.
hotels.description: Catalonia Santo Domingo is a modern 21-story tower set right on the Malecon (Avenida George Washington), giving all 228 rooms panoramic Caribbean Sea views and putting guests a short walk or drive from the Zona Colonial. It is one of the city's most amenity-rich hotels, with a large second-floor casino, a seventh-floor pool overlooking the sea, several restaurants and bars including a Spanish tapas bar, and an adults-only Premium Lounge on the 20th floor. The seafront location delivers sunsets and a lively boardwalk scene, though Santo Domingo's Malecon is an urban promenade rather than a swimmable beach. The high-rise format and busy public spaces suit travelers who want city energy, ocean views, and entertainment over an intimate stay.

Prime Malecon location, walkable toward Zona Colonial

hotels.name: Hodelpa Nicolas de Ovando - MGallery
hotels.summary: A characterful 5-star hotel set in the restored 16th-century home of a colonial governor in the heart of the Zona Colonial.
hotels.description: Hodelpa Nicolas de Ovando, part of Accor's MGallery collection, occupies the restored early-16th-century residence of governor Nicolas de Ovando on Calle Las Damas, the oldest paved street in the Americas, within the UNESCO-listed Colonial Zone. Its 91 rooms blend centuries-old stone walls and colonial architecture with contemporary comforts and L'Occitane amenities, and a tranquil courtyard pool framed by ancient walls is a rare find in the historic core. The location is unbeatable for history and culture, steps from the cathedral, Alcazar de Colon, and the riverfront, though the heritage building means some rooms are compact or have limited natural light, and views vary considerably by room category.

Atmospheric courtyard pool within centuries-old stone walls

hotels.summary: A design-forward boutique hotel of 26 suites in a restored 17th-century Colonial Zone building, topped by a rooftop pool.
hotels.description: Billini Hotel is an avant-garde boutique property in the Zona Colonial that pairs original 17th-century architecture with sleek modern design across roughly two dozen suites plus an apartment and a villa. Intimate and stylish, it features a rooftop pool and bar (Moon Restaurant & Bar) with views over the colonial rooftops, a spa, and additional dining at the Castil Restaurant, and sits within easy walking distance of the cathedral and Calle El Conde shopping street. Its small scale delivers personalized service and a chic atmosphere that appeals to design-minded couples, though the rooftop pool keeps limited hours and the historic location means surrounding streets can be lively and noisy at night.

hotels.name: Casas del XVI
hotels.summary: An ultra-intimate luxury hotel of 10 rooms spread across beautifully restored 16th-century houses in the Colonial Zone.
hotels.description: Casas del XVI is a collection of meticulously restored 16th-century colonial homes in the heart of the Zona Colonial, unified into a single boutique hotel of just ten individually designed rooms across houses such as Casa de los Mapas, Casa Macoris, Casa del Disenador, and Casa del Arbol. A few steps from the Santo Domingo Cathedral and Parque Colon, it offers a residential, almost private-villa atmosphere with palm-shaded courtyards, small pools, organic wellness treatments, and attentive mayordomo (butler) service tailored to couples and discerning travelers. The dispersed-house layout and tiny room count make it one of the city's most exclusive and romantic stays; the flip side is that amenities are intentionally minimal, there is no single central lobby or restaurant, and the high nightly rate reflects the bespoke, intimate experience.

hotels.name: Casa Bonita Tropical Lodge & Spa
hotels.summary: A romantic, family-run cliffside eco-luxury boutique lodge with sweeping ocean and jungle views in the unspoiled southwest.
hotels.description: Casa Bonita Tropical Lodge is a boutique retreat run by the third generation of the Schiffino family, perched on a forested hillside above the Caribbean at Km 17 Carretera de la Costa near La Cienaga, south of Barahona town. A member of Small Luxury Hotels of the World, it blends genuine eco credentials with refined comfort: palm-wood and wicker rooms, private balconies over the jungle, an infinity pool with sea views, and the Tanama Eco-Spa set among the trees beside a river. The Las Canas restaurant serves modern Dominican farm-to-table cuisine using produce from the on-site organic garden and the local community.

hotels.name: Hotel El Quemaito - Luxury Oceanfront Retreat
hotels.summary: A clifftop oceanfront retreat at Juan Esteban with two sea-view pools and a relaxed European-influenced restaurant.
hotels.description: Hotel El Quemaito sits on a low cliff at Juan Esteban Km 10, just south of Barahona town, offering 24 sea-view rooms and two in-ground pools that look out over the Caribbean. The restaurant mixes Caribbean, French and Italian cooking and serves a daily continental breakfast, giving the property a quiet, European-run guesthouse feel. It is a practical, good-value oceanfront base for exploring the Larimar coast, San Rafael's river-fed beach, and trips toward Bahia de las Aguilas.

hotels.name: Hotel Costa Larimar
hotels.summary: The largest conventional beach hotel in Barahona town, a practical full-service family and business base on the Caribbean shore.
hotels.description: Hotel Costa Larimar is Barahona's biggest mainstream hotel, with 108 rooms right on the shore of the Caribbean on Avenida Enriquillo, a short walk from Central Park and the malecon. It offers two pools (one for adults, one for children), a pool bar, an open-air international restaurant, and conference facilities, making it popular with both families and business and group travelers. It is functional and well located rather than glamorous - a reliable city-edge beach base for touring the southwest.

hotels.description: Hotel Playazul is an intimate clifftop property on the coastal road about 7 km south of Barahona, prized by guests for its dramatic ocean views, clean spacious rooms, attentive service and a pool that is a favorite afternoon spot. It has around 20 rooms across standard, family and superior categories, all air-conditioned with cable TV and safes. The trade-off of the cliff location is that swimming is mainly at the pool and the small private beach below, with the views the main draw.

hotels.name: Platon Ecolodge (Rancho Platon)
hotels.summary: A remote mountain eco-lodge above Paraiso with tree-house cabins, waterfalls and river tubing deep in the forest.
hotels.description: Platon Ecolodge, also known as Rancho Platon, is a boutique eco-lodge set in the mountains above the town of Paraiso, roughly 38 km from Barahona, with around 9 rooms spread across four lodges among trees, waterfalls and natural pools. Activities are nature-first: river tubing, hiking to waterfalls, horse riding, and relaxing by a natural swimming pool surrounded by cacao farms and forest. It is genuinely off-grid - reaching it requires a 4x4 or a pickup from town - which is part of its appeal for travelers seeking immersive eco-tourism.

hotels.summary: An intimate 8-room beachfront boutique club on Saladilla Beach with a pool, hot tub and sea-view suites.
hotels.description: Hotel La Saladilla Beach Club is a small, privacy-focused 8-room property set right on Saladilla Beach south of Barahona, with a white-sand cove and coral reef offshore. Rooms and suites feature sea views, flat-screen TVs and spacious terraces, and the property adds an outdoor pool and a hot tub plus an on-site restaurant praised for quality food. The intimate scale is the appeal, though guests should know the beach is small and not ideal for swimming, and reviews on cleanliness can be mixed.
